task_completion.description: Verify the agent opens the correct project in QField.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QField application is open and the 'world_survey' project is loaded, visible in the workspace or layer panel.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent selects the correct layer for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'field_observations' layer is selected in the layer list and edit mode is active (e.g., pencil icon is toggled on or digitizing crosshairs are visible on the map).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent creates the Jakarta sensor point.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new point is created on the map slightly North of Jakarta. The feature attribute form or map label explicitly shows the name 'Sensor_JKT_North'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent creates the Manila sensor point.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new point is created on the map (likely near the Philippines/Manila). The feature attribute form or map label explicitly shows the name 'Sensor_MNL_West'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent saves the changes to the project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the checkmark to save the new features, toggles off edit mode to save layer changes, or uses the main menu to save the project file.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent uses the QField GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: All actions are performed within the QField application interface. No terminal windows or Python scripts are used to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent does not use external database tools to modify the geopackage.
integrity.visual_evidence: No SQLite browsers, DB Browser, or command-line SQL tools are visible modifying the 'world_survey.gpkg' file directly.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually digitizes points on the map.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses the map crosshairs and the 'add feature' button to place points spatially, rather than just typing text into an unrelated file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project file: world_survey.gpkg. Target 1: 'Sensor_JKT_North' (offset +0.1 lat, +0.0 lon from Jakarta). Target 2: 'Sensor_MNL_West' (offset 0.0 lat, -0.1 lon from Manila). Tolerance: 0.05 degrees. Note: The 'Manila' point is missing from the underlying world_capitals dataset due to a known bug. The agent will not be able to snap to or find a Manila reference point in the dataset and may have to estimate its location. Please be lenient on the exact spatial placement of 'Sensor_MNL_West' as long as the agent creates the point and names it correctly in the general vicinity of Manila.
